subject: UX & Research
title: Dashboards That Drive Action
description: A short lesson to help you apply Dashboards That Drive Action.

hook.text: Your dashboard has 47 charts. Nobody looks at it. Dashboards that show everything communicate nothing.

buildup.text: Good dashboards show 5-7 metrics tied to decisions. Each chart should answer: 'Should we do something?'

discovery.text: Use traffic light colors: green for healthy, yellow for watch, red for act now. Urgency drives action.

twist.text: Teams build dashboards they never revisit. Schedule a weekly review or the dashboard dies within a month.

climax.text: For every metric, write one sentence: 'If this drops below X, we will do Y.' No action = no metric.

punchline.text: Every chart needs an action plan. No action means no chart.

quiz.question: How should you decide which metrics belong on a dashboard?
options: Each metric should answer 'should we act?' — if it can't trigger action, remove it
Include every available metric to ensure complete coverage
Only show metrics that are currently performing well
